At the end of the last week inside, I had that kind of, okay, what's next buzz? And, you know, it just doesn't work out here.
Life on the outside is pretty different.
Life on the outside is worse than expected.
Life on the outside is worse than life on the inside.
The offer to deal is there.
I have a lot of contacts.
I've been in the system, so lots of context.
If I do that again, the next leg is a long one. A real long one.
I don't think it'll work the way I always dream it will.
It never lasts.
I've been at a crossroads for 12 years.
There have been times when those crossroads would be more focused than other times.
Heaps of opportunities to start dealing again.
The different world isn't offering me opportunities.
The different world is offering me a night shift, and minimum wage, and, you know, nothing.
I've been fighting that system.
All it takes me back is to the inside.
Minimum wage night shift at a petrol station doesn't look very good.
Night shift at a petrol station looks really hard.
Eventually you have to go off to your job and pay your mortgage, and it's just massive.
All my mates are in Australia working awesome jobs, mining for 160K a year, you know? Just being men digging holes.
I have just come out of prison, I don't have any NCEA.
Maybe the world is giving me opportunities and I just don't need them.
I had one opportunity and I fucked that because I can't do night shift 'cause I'm lazy.
I don't have qualifications.
Maybe I can't do that.
People like me are unaccepted into that world though.
You know, I can make in an hour what I can make in a night or a week of nights.
I just said, "I won't yet".
harm_effect: I know that's the right thing, uh, don't get me wrong.
Going to a job you hate, going home, cooking your missus a dinner, looking after your kids is what a real man does, but I don't have those guts.

That is gut right there, going to a job you hate, going home, cooking yet missus a dinner, looking after your kids. I mean, that is-- That's what it real mean does, but I don't have that-- Th-those guts to do- to do what you do.

detail: I haven't gone back to dealing yet.
The offer to deal is there.
I don't know why I haven't said that I will yet.
I've been out for three weeks and one day.
I kind of told myself not to before I left.
I've made a decision not to pick up my old life and continue on.
I can't go back there. There's no way I'm going back there.
I've made a commitment 22 days, drug-free.
I just told myself I wouldn't this time and then Fiona said, "You will not" this time.
I'm the sort of guy that once I make a decision, I'm staunch with that decision, even if it's the wrong one.
I just said, "I won't yet".
He was inside with me the first time I was ever inside, and now he's a drug counselor.
He did it.
I don't know how he did it, but he wasn't even a real addict.
He's very good with people.
Really good at talking.
effect: I can't ever be a drug counselor.
I think he would have found some higher power. They find-- I'm not gonna find one of them.

detail: I just never did make my own opportunity in life.
You make your own opportunity in life, don't you?
effect: Sadness about missed opportunities.
Opportunities come and go all the time, but not to me.

detail: Waking up, having a cup of tea, and sitting on the porch is kind of cool.
It is the little things, yeah, like waking up, and having a cup of tea and sitting on the porch is kind of cool.
No one sits on the porch with me in the morning, it's just me.
Birds, trees, yada yada.
effect: The world actually has the freedom to be my own man.

detail: She has stayed with me through all the difficulties, and I owe her.
Fiona has told me that it's over if I deal again.
She's the one who stayed through all the shit.
I've been with Fiona for 12 years.
She's actually got the black and white line down now.
She could be pretty full-on.
It's hard to ignore that line.
It comes with a whole lot of different rules, and she's never been quite like she is now.
I think she has changed, but I don't think I have.
Something's changed in our relationship.
She just wants me. I mean, she just wants someone to be there. Me. She-she remembers me and she wants- she wants him.
She wants the version of me she fell in love with and saw great things in.
No one would want the version of me that keeps getting into trouble, not even me.
No one would want him. I don't want him. He's a fuckwit, right? She wants the one that she fell in love with.

content: Reclaiming the Daryl Fiona fell in love with
detail: I wish I knew where to start to give her that version of me.
I don't know how to start reclaiming the Daryl Fiona fell in love with.
I was pretty romantic. I mean, I was pretty quite smooth, and, um, you know, I was on top of my game, like, she- and she loved it.
We were young. How do you become young? Uh, you know, I'm old, and we'll keep growing old, but you know?

We used to laugh in the , you know? And just- it was always really sweet, and people used to say, "Oh, man, you two. You two have just got it," you know? And we lost it, so whatever that was, that's what she wants.
confidence_to_achive: I don't know how to achieve this.

detail: Fiona offers suggestions on what I could do, but they are not the things I need.
I need to come up with something because it's like awesome love, but not in love, and she's getting sick of it already.
I started placating her and making it okay to keep giving me suggestions.
Things are starting to get tense at home.
She hasn't even talked about the dealing, like, she won't even broach that subject. That's how black and white she is.
Tense is the right word to describe the current situation at home.
I haven't got little, um, nuggets of where I could go. I've just got like this feeling that I should go somewhere.
confidence_to_achive: A part of me knows I need to do something different, but the direction isn't clear.
I don't know what might work for me, that's why I'm here.
I need fucking guts to go there.
I'd need some of them.
I don't have the guts yet.
I don't have that much courage.
I have to be here.
Maybe I have some courage to think about these ideas.

detail: I kind of want to be convinced.
No. You know, convince me because, you know, I kind of want to be.
I'm definitely not convinced.

content: Taking first steps into a pro-social life
detail: It is obviously about first steps, but I don't know what the first step into a pro-social life is.
If I get a job that I hate, I just won't do it.

You know, I can make in an hour what I can make in a night or a week of nights. It's pretty-- You know, from where I'm from that a really-- You know, there's no decision at all, that's just like, what are you up to if you don't?

target.speaker: Counselor
target.concat_utterance: Yeah, okay, so that will be a call you-you've got to make.

n_contexts.concat_utterance: -at the moment, is what I'm picking up.

I don't think it'll work the way I always dream it will.
It never lasts.

I've been fighting that system.
All it takes me back is to the inside.
Minimum wage night shift at a petrol station doesn't look very good.

Maybe the world is giving me opportunities and I just don't need them.

I don't have qualifications.
Maybe I can't do that.

I don't know why I haven't said that I will yet.

I can't go back there. There's no way I'm going back there.
I've made a commitment 22 days, drug-free.
I just told myself I wouldn't this time and then Fiona said, "You will not" this time.

I just said, "I won't yet".

He did it.
I don't know how he did it, but he wasn't even a real addict.

Really good at talking.
effect: I can't ever be a drug counselor.

detail: Waking up, having a cup of tea, and sitting on the porch is kind of cool.

No one sits on the porch with me in the morning, it's just me.
Birds, trees, yada yada.
effect: The world actually has the freedom to be my own man.

detail: She has stayed with me through all the difficulties, and I owe her.
Fiona has told me that it's over if I deal again.
She's the one who stayed through all the shit.
I've been with Fiona for 12 years.
She's actually got the black and white line down now.
She could be pretty full-on.
It's hard to ignore that line.
It comes with a whole lot of different rules, and she's never been quite like she is now.
I think she has changed, but I don't think I have.
Something's changed in our relationship.
She just wants me. I mean, she just wants someone to be there. Me. She-she remembers me and she wants- she wants him.
She wants the version of me she fell in love with and saw great things in.
No one would want the version of me that keeps getting into trouble, not even me.
No one would want him. I don't want him. He's a fuckwit, right? She wants the one that she fell in love with.

It would be pretty odd calling, but he'd probably be okay with it.
It was a long time ago that we were inside together, and I just know that's what he's doing.
Possibility to call.
confidence_to_achive: Possibility to call.
Possibility to call, yeah.

detail: Fiona has more hopes than me.
I do think about her hopes, but it's scary to consider what it would take to share them.
I would never admit to that.
I do but what it would take, you know, I mean, that's pretty freaking scary shit.
I was being chipping, I mean, yeah she has more hopes than me, you know.

target.speaker: Counselor
target.concat_utterance: So if you made a decision to go there, it might be a scary as shit but again it seems that, uh, it could work if see it look, this is important, this relationship's important, let me go for—

n_contexts.speaker: Client
n_contexts.concat_utterance: Um, like I do- I do but what it would take, you know, I mean, that's pretty freaking scary shit.

n_contexts.speaker: Client
n_contexts.concat_utterance: Yeah. And I would never admit to that.

n_contexts.speaker: Counselor
n_contexts.concat_utterance: You said before you're sort of guy that once you make a decision you're pretty staunch with that decision.

content: I've been thinking about how easy it would be to deal P again.
I've been thinking about how I could actually set myself up the right way doing it the wrong way.

detail: I've been thinking about how easy it would be to deal P again.
I've been thinking about how I could actually set myself up the right way doing it the wrong way.
At the end of the last week inside, I had that kind of, okay, what's next buzz? And, you know, it just doesn't work out here.
Life on the outside is pretty different.
Life on the outside is worse than expected.
Life on the outside is worse than life on the inside.
The offer to deal is there.
I have a lot of contacts.
I've been in the system, so lots of context.
If I do that again, the next leg is a long one. A real long one.
I don't think it'll work the way I always dream it will.
It never lasts.
I've been at a crossroads for 12 years.
There have been times when those crossroads would be more focused than other times.
Heaps of opportunities to start dealing again.
The different world isn't offering me opportunities.
The different world is offering me a night shift, and minimum wage, and, you know, nothing.
I've been fighting that system.
All it takes me back is to the inside.
Minimum wage night shift at a petrol station doesn't look very good.
Night shift at a petrol station looks really hard.
Eventually you have to go off to your job and pay your mortgage, and it's just massive.
All my mates are in Australia working awesome jobs, mining for 160K a year, you know? Just being men digging holes.
I have just come out of prison, I don't have any NCEA.
Maybe the world is giving me opportunities and I just don't need them.
I had one opportunity and I fucked that because I can't do night shift 'cause I'm lazy.
I don't have qualifications.
Maybe I can't do that.
People like me are unaccepted into that world though.
You know, I can make in an hour what I can make in a night or a week of nights.
I just said, "I won't yet".
I hate that.
harm_effect: I know that's the right thing, uh, don't get me wrong.
Going to a job you hate, going home, cooking your missus a dinner, looking after your kids is what a real man does, but I don't have those guts.

That is gut right there, going to a job you hate, going home, cooking yet missus a dinner, looking after your kids. I mean, that is-- That's what it real mean does, but I don't have that-- Th-those guts to do- to do what you do.
necessity_to_improve: Engaging in this transition into a different life with Fiona.

detail: I haven't gone back to dealing yet.
The offer to deal is there.
I don't know why I haven't said that I will yet.
I've been out for three weeks and one day.
I kind of told myself not to before I left.
I've made a decision not to pick up my old life and continue on.
I can't go back there. There's no way I'm going back there.
I've made a commitment 22 days, drug-free.
I just told myself I wouldn't this time and then Fiona said, "You will not" this time.
I'm the sort of guy that once I make a decision, I'm staunch with that decision, even if it's the wrong one.
I just said, "I won't yet".
He was inside with me the first time I was ever inside, and now he's a drug counselor.
He did it.
I don't know how he did it, but he wasn't even a real addict.
He's very good with people.
Really good at talking.
effect: I can't ever be a drug counselor.
I think he would have found some higher power. They find-- I'm not gonna find one of them.
12-step stuff works for him.

detail: I just never did make my own opportunity in life.
You make your own opportunity in life, don't you?
effect: Sadness about missed opportunities.
Opportunities come and go all the time, but not to me.

detail: Waking up, having a cup of tea, and sitting on the porch is kind of cool.
It is the little things, yeah, like waking up, and having a cup of tea and sitting on the porch is kind of cool.
No one sits on the porch with me in the morning, it's just me.
Birds, trees, yada yada.
effect: The world actually has the freedom to be my own man.

detail: She has stayed with me through all the difficulties, and I owe her.
Fiona has told me that it's over if I deal again.
She's the one who stayed through all the shit.
I've been with Fiona for 12 years.
She's actually got the black and white line down now.
She could be pretty full-on.
It's hard to ignore that line.
It comes with a whole lot of different rules, and she's never been quite like she is now.
I think she has changed, but I don't think I have.
Something's changed in our relationship.
She just wants me. I mean, she just wants someone to be there. Me. She-she remembers me and she wants- she wants him.
She wants the version of me she fell in love with and saw great things in.
No one would want the version of me that keeps getting into trouble, not even me.
No one would want him. I don't want him. He's a fuckwit, right? She wants the one that she fell in love with.

content: Reclaiming the Daryl Fiona fell in love with
detail: I wish I knew where to start to give her that version of me.
I don't know how to start reclaiming the Daryl Fiona fell in love with.
I was pretty romantic. I mean, I was pretty quite smooth, and, um, you know, I was on top of my game, like, she- and she loved it.
We were young. How do you become young? Uh, you know, I'm old, and we'll keep growing old, but you know?

We used to laugh in the , you know? And just- it was always really sweet, and people used to say, "Oh, man, you two. You two have just got it," you know? And we lost it, so whatever that was, that's what she wants.
confidence_to_achive: I don't know how to achieve this.

detail: Fiona offers suggestions on what I could do, but they are not the things I need.
I need to come up with something because it's like awesome love, but not in love, and she's getting sick of it already.
I started placating her and making it okay to keep giving me suggestions.
Things are starting to get tense at home.
She hasn't even talked about the dealing, like, she won't even broach that subject. That's how black and white she is.
Tense is the right word to describe the current situation at home.
I haven't got little, um, nuggets of where I could go. I've just got like this feeling that I should go somewhere.
confidence_to_achive: A part of me knows I need to do something different, but the direction isn't clear.
I don't know what might work for me, that's why I'm here.
I need fucking guts to go there.
I'd need some of them.
I don't have the guts yet.
I don't have that much courage.
I have to be here.
Maybe I have some courage to think about these ideas.

detail: I kind of want to be convinced.
No. You know, convince me because, you know, I kind of want to be.
I'm definitely not convinced.

content: Taking first steps into a pro-social life
detail: It is obviously about first steps, but I don't know what the first step into a pro-social life is.
If I get a job that I hate, I just won't do it.

You know, I can make in an hour what I can make in a night or a week of nights. It's pretty-- You know, from where I'm from that a really-- You know, there's no decision at all, that's just like, what are you up to if you don't?

content: Engaging in a transition into a different life with Fiona
detail: Engaging in this transition into a different life with Fiona.
Discussed position for about 10 hours with the counselor.
No discussion with anyone other than Fiona.

It does seem exciting because it's so different.
It does kinda seem exciting.
